title: 27 Contingency Procedures for Incoming and Outgoing Wires
body_text:
27	Contingency Procedures for Incoming and Outgoing Wires
27.1	Overview of Contingency Procedures for Incoming and Outgoing Wires
If your access to Wires is unavailable, your designated contingency processor sends and receives wires on your behalf. Settlement between your financial institution and your provincial central is processed normally.
These procedures apply to localized contingency situations only (e.g., your financial institution can芒聙聶t access the internet). Contingency procedures that address a failure of the Wires service aren芒聙聶t provided. Wires will automatically run on its backup servers if the main servers are disrupted. Incoming and outgoing wires are processed normally.
27.2	Designated Contingency Processors
Use the following table to identify your contingency processor.
Financial Institution
Designated Contingency Processor
Description
BC and Ontario Credit Unions, and other Central 1 clients
Central 1
Payments & DBPX
Electronic Funds Transfers Team
Phone:
604-737-5039/5040/5041
Toll-free:
1-800-661-6813
Fax:
604-730-4408
Central 1 forwards incoming wires to the receiving financial institution.
The sending financial institution forwards outgoing wires to Central 1. Two signing officers on file with Central 1 must authorize each request.
Manitoba Credit Unions
Banking Operations
CUCM
Fax:
204-943-5034
Email:
banking@cucm.org
CUCM forwards incoming wires to the receiving credit union.
The sending credit union forwards outgoing wires to CUCM. Two signing officers on file with CUCM must authorize each request.
Manitoba Caisses Populaires
Directeur ou Adjoint au Directeur Services Op脙漏rations et Gestion R脙漏seau
Caisse Groupe Financier
Fax:
204-233-6405
CUCM forwards incoming wires to the Caisse Groupe.
The sending caisse populaire forwards outgoing wires to the Caisse Groupe. Two signing officers on file with the Caisse Groupe must authorize each request.
Atlantic Credit Unions
Atlantic Central
Fax:
902-454-3114
Email:
payments@aclsm.ca
Atlantic Central forwards incoming wires to the receiving credit union.
The sending credit union forwards outgoing wires to Atlantic Central. Two signing officers on file with Atlantic Central must authorize each request.
27.3	Creating Outgoing Wires in a Contingency Situation
To create an outgoing wire if your financial institution is unable to access the Wires application:
Important
:	Outgoing wires created in contingency situations are subject to the rules and regulations described in previous chapters.
Note:	Don芒聙聶t initiate contingency processing procedures if the expected outage time is minimal.
Contact Your Contingency Processor
芒聙聯
Advise your designated contingency processor that you can芒聙聶t access Wires and are implementing contingency processing.
Prepare the Outgoing Wire
芒聙聯
See Chapter 14, Considerations for Outgoing Wires, and Chapter 15, Preparing Outgoing Wires.
Complete the Contingency Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1799
芒聙聯 Proceed as follows. See Figure 1, Figure 2, and Figure 3. Fields with an asterisk (*) are required.
芒聭
Wire Amount and Currency
芒聢聮
Date and Time
芒聙聯 Record the date and time that the outgoing wire was faxed to your designated contingency processor.
芒聢聮
Wire Currency
芒聙聯 Record the currency type (e.g., CAD, USD, other).
芒聢聮
Convert Funds to Currency of Country
芒聙聯 Select the check box to send a wire in a foreign currency if the exchange rate isn芒聙聶t known. Record the Canadian dollar amount in the Wire Amount field. Record the foreign currency type beside the Other field.
芒聢聮
Payable to Recipient Upon Identification
芒聙聯 Select the check box if the beneficiary isn芒聙聶t a client of the receiving financial institution and funds are released upon presentation of the beneficiary芒聙聶s identification.
芒聢聮
Sending Clerk Name
芒聙聯 Record the name of the sending clerk.
芒聢聮
Wire Amount
芒聙聯 Record the amount of the outgoing wire.
Note:	The wire will be created according to the amount recorded in the Wire Amount field.
芒聢聮
Wire Currency
芒聙聯 Record the currency type (e.g., CAD, USD, EUR).
芒聢聮
Exchange Rate
芒聙聯 If applicable, record the exchange rate.
芒聢聮
Receiving Commission
芒聙聯 Not applicable unless the sender wants to include the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s fees in the amount of the outgoing wire and the amount of the fees is known.
芒聢聮
Charges
芒聙聯 Record your charge and the pass-through charge.
芒聢聮
Other
芒聙聯 Record any other fees, if applicable.
芒聢聮
Total
芒聙聯 Record the total amount that will be charged to the sender.
芒聭隆
Sender Information
芒聢聮
Financial Institution Details
芒聙聯 Record the name, transit number, address, and phone number of your financial institution.
芒聢聮
Sender Details
芒聙聯 Record the customer type, account number, customer number, name, and full address of the sender (ordering customer).
芒聢聮
Foreign Currency Exchange Transaction Details
芒聙聯 If the outgoing wire is purchased with a currency other than that in which it芒聙聶s sent, record the currency of purchase, the medium of exchange of the purchase (e.g., cash, cheques), and the amount of the purchase.
芒聢聮
Additional Information for Outgoing Wires of $1,000 (Canadian Equivalent) or More
芒聙聯 Record the sender芒聙聶s occupation or nature of principal business, date of birth, phone number, identification type, and identification number. This information doesn芒聙聶t have to be recorded on the form if the sender is an account holder and the required information is recorded in the account documentation.
芒聢聮
Wire Funds
芒聙聯 Record how the sender paid for the wire. Record if information about the source of funds (person/entity) was obtained. If yes, record how the requester initially obtained the funds. Record if the transaction is face-to-face. Canadian law requires specific methods for determining the identity of the sender in non-face-to-face transactions. See FINTRAC芒聙聶s website for information.
芒聢聮
Politically Exposed Person (PEP) / Head of International Organization (HIO) Declaration
芒聙聯 Make a PEP/HIO determination on outgoing wires greater than the equivalent of $100,000 Canadian within 14 days of sending the wire. The PEP/HIO determination must be made on individuals conducting transactions on personal accounts and on individuals conducting transactions on business accounts. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Figure 1: Contingency Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1799, Page 1
芒聭垄
Beneficiary Information
芒聢聮
Financial Institution Details
芒聙聯
Record the name, routing information (transit number, ABA number, or SWIFT code), address, and phone number of the receiving financial institution.
芒聢聮
Beneficiary Details
芒聙聯
Record the customer type, account number (or IBAN if applicable), Sort Code (if applicable), name, and full address of the beneficiary.
Note:	If the outgoing wire is directed to an IBAN-regulated country, obtain the IBAN from the sender and record it in the Account No. field. See Section 14.3, IBAN Requirements, for more information.
芒聭拢
Intermediary Financial Institution Information
芒聢聮
Record the intermediary financial institution芒聙聶s SWIFT ID and, if known, the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s account number with the intermediary. Or record the name and address of the intermediary financial institution and, if known, the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s account number with the intermediary.
Important
:	You must obtain the intermediary information from the sender when sending Canadian dollars to the US. Record the intermediary information the sender provides on the form. Central 1 can芒聙聶t provide the intermediary information.
All international outgoing wires are processed through intermediary financial institutions that can deduct fees from the wire. Central 1 doesn芒聙聶t control the fees other financial institutions charge.
芒聭陇
Additional Payment Details
Complete this section to include additional information for the beneficiary (e.g., an invoice number).
芒聭楼
Sender Financial Institution to Receiver Financial Institution Information
Complete this section to include additional information for the beneficiary芒聙聶s financial institution.
Figure 2: Contingency Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1799, Page 2
芒聭娄
Additional Information for the FINTRAC EFT Report
Information recorded in this section can be included in the FINTRAC EFT Report (if applicable). Make a reasonable effort to complete this section if an international outgoing wire is $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more.
If the financial institution is aware of multiple wires conducted by the same person that, in total, equal or exceed $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) within 24 hours, the financial institution must report the wires to FINTRAC.
Note:	The financial institution is responsible for analysis of the data and for reporting wires to FINTRAC.
芒聢聮
Sender (Ordering Customer) Details
芒聙聯
Make a reasonable effort to record the sender芒聙聶s phone number and, if the sender is an individual, their date of birth, occupation, ID type, and ID number. A reasonable effort includes getting this information from the account documentation.
芒聢聮
Beneficiary Customer Info
芒聙聯 Make a reasonable effort to record the beneficiary芒聙聶s phone number and, if the beneficiary is an individual, their date of birth, occupation, and ID type.
芒聢聮
Sender (Ordering Customer) Third Party Information and Beneficiary Customer Third Party Information
芒聙聯 Make a reasonable effort to determine if a third party is involved in sending or receiving the outgoing wire. If a third party is identified, select the
Yes
radio button beside 芒聙聹Is this wire being sent on behalf of a third party?芒聙聺 and/or 芒聙聹Will this wire be received on behalf of a third party?芒聙聺 Indicate if the third party is an individual or an entity. Make a reasonable effort to obtain the third party芒聙聶s name, address, date of birth (individuals only), occupation (individuals only), ID type (individuals only) and their relationship to the account holder.
芒聭搂
Sender Agreement
Have the sender sign the form and record the date.
Figure 3: Contingency Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1799, Page 3
芒聭篓
Financial Institution Authorization
Have two authorized signing officers sign the form.
Figure 4: Contingency Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1799, Page 4
Photocopy the Contingency Outgoing Wire Request
芒聙聯 Distribute copies as follows:
芒聢聮
Give one copy to the sender as a receipt.
芒聢聮
Retain one copy for your records. The R5 code in the bottom right-hand corner of the form indicates that the retention period is five years.
Fax the Contingency Outgoing Wire Request
芒聙聯 Fax the form to your designated contingency processor as indicated on the form. The designated contingency processor will provide confirmation of receipt and will enter the outgoing wire in the Wires application.
Important
:	It芒聙聶s your responsibility to ensure that your designated contingency processor receives the fax. Call your designated contingency processor if confirmation of receipt isn芒聙聶t received within two hours.
Submit a FINTRAC EFT Report (if applicable
)
芒聙聯
Large international outgoing wires must be reported to FINTRAC. This also applies if two or more transactions totalling $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more are sent within 24 hours (and the financial institution knows that the transactions are conducted by or on behalf of the same person).
Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Submit a FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Report
芒聙聯
If there are reasonable grounds to suspect that the outgoing wire is related to a money laundering or terrorist financing offence, submit a FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Report. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Submit a FINTRAC Terrorist Property Report
芒聙聯
If your financial institution knows or believes it has an outgoing wire in its possession or control that芒聙聶s owned or controlled by or on behalf of a terrorist, terrorist group, or listed person, then submit a FINTRAC Terrorist Property Report and freeze the funds. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Settlement
芒聙聯 Processed as described in Section 26.2, Settlement of Outgoing Wires.
Related Information
Contingency Outgoing Wire Request,
Form 1799
Politically Exposed Person Statement,
Form 2351
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
27.4	Receiving Incoming Wires in a Contingency Situation
To receive incoming wires if your financial institution is unable to access Wires:
Important
:	Incoming wires received in contingency situations are subject to the rules and regulations described in previous chapters.
Note:	Don芒聙聶t initiate contingency-processing procedures if the expected outage time is minimal.
Contact Your Contingency Processor
芒聙聯
Advise your designated contingency processor that you can芒聙聶t access Wires and are implementing contingency processing.
Obtain the Wire Details
芒聙聯
Check your fax machine for incoming wires regularly. Your designated contingency processor will fax them to you. See sample of the Wires Fax Notification, Form 2442, in Figure 5.
Figure 5: Wire Fax Notification, Form 2442
Verify the Receiving Financial Institution
芒聙聯
Verify that the incoming wire is for your financial institution. Contact your designated contingency processor immediately if the incoming wire was sent to you in error.
Verify the Beneficiary Details
芒聙聯
Confirm that the name and account number on the incoming wire match the name and account number in your records.
Important
:	Further to CPA By-law No. 7, section 49, Identification of Payee, if a financial institution relies on the account number to credit the beneficiary and doesn芒聙聶t notice a discrepancy between the name on the account and the name in the payment message, it has satisfied its obligations to the beneficiary. If you notice that the name on the wire differs from the name on the account, don芒聙聶t credit the beneficiary. The finality of payment may not apply. Either issue a trace request or return the incoming wire to the sending financial institution.
Determine if the Ordering Customer Information is Missing
芒聙聯
Determine if the ordering customer芒聙聶s name, address, and, if applicable, account number or reference number are included in the details of all domestic and international incoming wires, regardless of the amount.
If you aren芒聙聶t required to submit a FINTRAC EFT Report for an incoming wire and the ordering customer芒聙聶s name, address, and/or account number or reference number, if applicable, are missing, make a reasonable
effort to obtain the missing information. If the missing information is obtained, it can be recorded on a printout of the incoming wire. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
If a FINTRAC EFT Report will be submitted to FINTRAC, the report must include the originator芒聙聶s name and, if applicable, the account or reference number. A FINTRAC EFT Report must be submitted for Large International incoming wire if
芒聢聮
your financial institution is the first Canadian financial institution to receive the wire, or
芒聢聮
the beneficiary芒聙聶s name and/or address are missing, even if your financial institution isn芒聙聶t the first Canadian financial institution to receive the wire.
If the originator芒聙聶s information is missing, obtain the missing information and submit the FINTRAC EFT Report to FINTRAC. Initiate a trace to obtain the information.
Important
:	The incoming wire must be reported to FINTRAC, even if it芒聙聶s returned to the sending financial institution.
Instruct the Contingency Processor to Accept or Return the Wire
芒聢聮
Write 芒聙聹Accept芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹Return芒聙聺 on the printout of the incoming wire. Your designated contingency processor will accept or return the incoming wire in Wires according to your instructions.
芒聢聮
Ask two signing officers to sign the printout of the incoming wire.
芒聢聮
Fax the authorized printout of the incoming wire to your designated contingency processor. Retain a copy for your records.
Report International Incoming Wires of $10,000 (Canadian Equivalent) or More to FINTRAC
.
Submit the FINTRAC EFT Report to FINTRAC within five business days of receipt of the incoming wire. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Submit a FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Report
if there are reasonable grounds to believe that the incoming wire is related to a money laundering or terrorist financing offence. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Important
:	A financial institution can芒聙聶t disclose to an individual that it will submit a FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Report.
Submit a FINTRAC Terrorist Property Report
if your financial institution knows or believes it has an incoming wire in its possession or control that芒聙聶s owned or controlled by or on behalf of a terrorist, terrorist group, or listed person. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Make a PEP Determination
w
ithin 14 days of receipt of a very large international wire if the beneficiary is an individual. A PEP determination isn芒聙聶t required if the beneficiary is an entity. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Complete a Foreign Exchange Transaction Ticket
if an incoming wire is exchanged into a currency other than that in which it was received. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Identify and Credit the Beneficiary
芒聙聯 Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Settlement
芒聙聯
Processed as described in Section 26.3, Settlement of Incoming Wires.
Related Information
Foreign Exchange Transaction Ticket,
Form 1923
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
Politically Exposed Person Statement,
Form 2351
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
CPA By-law No. 7, section 49, Identification of Payee (
www.payments.ca/
)
END
